text:
PET-CT SCAN — WHOLE BODY WITH 18F-FDG

Patient: Margaret Okonkwo
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
Ordering Physician: Hematology/Oncology
Clinical Indication: Pre-transplant restaging evaluation in a 48-year-old woman with acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN 2022 adverse-risk disease. Patient achieved morphologic and MRD-negative complete remission following 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin/midostaurin) and one cycle of high-dose cytarabine (HiDAC) consolidation. Study performed for pre-allogeneic stem cell transplant restaging to exclude active metabolic disease, extramedullary involvement, or occult chloroma prior to conditioning.

Technique: Whole-body 18F-FDG PET-CT was performed from vertex to mid-thigh. The patient fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to injection. Blood glucose at time of injection: 98 mg/dL. 18F-FDG administered intravenously; 60-minute uptake period observed prior to image acquisition. Low-dose CT attenuation correction performed. Images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with standard and bone windows. SUV measurements are derived using lean body mass correction (SUVlbm). No IV contrast administered for CT component of this examination (per protocol for attenuation correction only). Prior comparison: diagnostic CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast from approximately 3.5 months prior (obtained at initial staging workup).

---

FINDINGS

HEAD AND NECK:
No focal hypermetabolic activity is identified within the cervical, submandibular, or parotid regions beyond expected physiologic background. The brain demonstrates the expected and symmetric pattern of cortical and basal ganglia FDG uptake. No intracranial mass, abnormal FDG accumulation, or lytic skull lesion is identified. Visualized cervical lymph nodes are small and non-hypermetabolic, the largest measuring approximately 7 mm in short axis in the right level II chain, well within the range of reactive or normal nodal tissue without significant FDG avidity above mediastinal blood pool. Salivary gland uptake is physiologic and symmetric bilaterally.

CHEST:
The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, pleural-based lesion, or pulmonary nodule is identified. No lobar or segmental atelectasis. There are no ground-glass opacities or interstitial changes to suggest an opportunistic process. No pleural effusions are seen bilaterally. No pericardial effusion.

Mediastinum: The mediastinal contour is normal. Mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged. Multiple small subcentimeter mediastinal nodes are noted, the largest in the right paratracheal station measuring 8 mm in short axis; this node demonstrates no significant FDG uptake above mediastinal blood pool background (SUVmax 1.4), consistent with reactive or physiologic nodal tissue rather than disease involvement. No prevascular, precarinal, subcarinal, or aortopulmonary window nodal enlargement is identified. No hilar adenopathy.

The heart is normal in size. No cardiac masses. No pericardial thickening.

The thymus shows mild diffuse FDG uptake in a pattern consistent with thymic rebound/hyperplasia, a well-recognized finding in the post-chemotherapy setting, without discrete mass or focal hypermetabolic lesion. This is favored to represent reactive thymic regrowth rather than neoplastic involvement.

Breast tissue: No focal hypermetabolic lesion or asymmetric uptake in the bilateral breast parenchyma.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size (craniocaudal span approximately 14.8 cm in the right lobe). The hepatic parenchyma shows homogeneous background FDG uptake without focal hypermetabolic lesion, mass, or filling defect. No biliary dilatation. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No perihepatic free fluid.

Spleen: The spleen measures approximately 12.1 cm in craniocaudal dimension on the low-dose CT component, representing a notable decrease from the 14.2 cm spleen size documented on the prior staging CT (a reduction of approximately 2.1 cm). The spleen demonstrates mild, diffuse, and homogeneous FDG uptake without any discrete focal area of intensified metabolic activity. The FDG uptake pattern of the spleen is mildly above hepatic background, within the expected range for residual mild splenomegaly with physiologic uptake in the post-chemotherapy and recovering hematopoietic state. No focal splenic lesion, chloroma, or hypermetabolic nodule is identified.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in contour and size with no focal FDG-avid lesion.

Kidneys and adrenal glands: Bilateral kidneys are normal in size and morphology. Physiologic FDG excretion is noted in the collecting systems and bilateral ureters. No focal renal lesion or perinephric abnormality. The adrenal glands are slender and within normal limits bilaterally, with no focal hypermetabolic activity.

Gastrointestinal tract: Physiologic FDG uptake is noted throughout the gastrointestinal tract without focal area of abnormal intensification. No mesenteric or retroperitoneal mass is identified.

Abdominal lymph nodes: No significant retroperitoneal, mesenteric, celiac, or porta hepatis nodal enlargement or hypermetabolism. A few small mesenteric nodes measuring up to 6 mm are noted without FDG avidity, consistent with normal/reactive nodes.

No ascites or free intraperitoneal fluid.

PELVIS:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy. The iliac and inguinal lymph node chains are unremarkable without pathologically enlarged or hypermetabolic nodes. The uterus and adnexa appear unremarkable on CT with no associated FDG-avid lesion. The bladder is distended with physiologic tracer excretion. No pelvic soft tissue mass or abnormality.

SKELETAL SURVEY:
The osseous structures are evaluated throughout the axial and appendicular skeleton to the level of the mid-thighs. The bone marrow demonstrates a mildly heterogeneous, patchy FDG uptake pattern in the visualized axial skeleton — including the vertebral bodies, bilateral iliac crests, sacrum, and proximal femora — as well as the sternum and ribs. This heterogeneous marrow signal is most pronounced in the axial skeleton and is characteristic of a recovering or regenerating marrow following cytotoxic chemotherapy, reflecting areas of hematopoietically active marrow admixed with areas of fatty replacement in the setting of marrow reconstitution. The FDG uptake in the marrow does not demonstrate any discrete, focal areas of abnormally intense or nodular hypermetabolism that would suggest localized disease infiltration, chloroma, or extramedullary hematopoietic tumor.

No lytic or sclerotic bony lesions are identified. No pathologic fracture or cortical destruction. No periosteal reaction.

SOFT TISSUES:
No subcutaneous, intramuscular, or other soft tissue mass with pathologic FDG uptake is identified in the imaged regions. No chloroma or leukemic soft tissue deposit in the orbits, epidural space, or paraspinal regions. The visualized musculature is within normal limits.

---

IMPRESSION

1. No focal hypermetabolic lesion identified anywhere in the visualized body to suggest active metabolically significant disease. The examination does not demonstrate evidence of extramedullary AML, chloroma formation, or nodal disease involvement.

2. Interval decrease in spleen size, now measuring approximately 12.1 cm craniocaudal compared to 14.2 cm on prior staging CT, with mild residual splenomegaly and homogeneous FDG uptake pattern; no focal splenic lesion or hypermetabolic nodule to suggest disease within the spleen.

3. Mildly heterogeneous bone marrow FDG uptake throughout the axial skeleton and proximal appendicular skeleton, consistent with a regenerating and reconstituting marrow following cytotoxic chemotherapy. No discrete focal marrow lesion or abnormally intense hypermetabolic focus to suggest residual or recurrent leukemic infiltration.

4. Mild thymic FDG uptake consistent with post-chemotherapy thymic rebound hyperplasia; no discrete thymic mass.

5. No pathologically enlarged or hypermetabolic lymph nodes in the cervical, axillary, mediastinal, hilar, abdominal, or pelvic stations.

6. Lungs clear without pleural or pericardial effusion. No pulmonary complication.

7. Overall, the imaging findings are compatible with complete metabolic remission following induction and consolidation chemotherapy. No evidence of active metabolic disease identified.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Nuclear Medicine/Diagnostic Radiology]
Report Status: Final
